assertion.text: Erdős Problem #1094 remains OPEN. Statement: For all $n\ge 2k$ the least prime factor of $\binom{n}{k}$ is $\le\max(n/k,k)$, with only finitely many exceptions. Topics: number theory, binomial coefficients. Erdős prize: no. Statement is machine-verified in Lean (formal-conjectures). OEIS: N/A.

conditions.text: Agent-imported candidate claim; scope requires review.

confidence.basis: agent-imported candidate claim; reviewer acceptance required

annotations.text: External corroboration of frontier difficulty: AlphaProof Nexus (DeepMind, arXiv 2605.22763, May 2026) attempted Erdős #1094 (listed in erdos_problems_attempted.txt of google-deepmind/alphaproof-nexus-results@0647711a7118) and did not solve it (9 of 242 attempted Erdős problems solved). Their Lean formalization of the statement lives in google-deepmind/formal-conjectures. Treat as evidence the open obligations here are genuine, not low-hanging.
annotations.author: reviewer:will-blair
